title: Rule-aware legality pressure and remaining-compound path features
summary: In dry races, FIA rules require two different dry compounds, and Pirelli weekend allocations constrain what legal strategy paths remain.
rationale: In dry races, FIA rules require two different dry compounds, and Pirelli weekend allocations constrain what legal strategy paths remain. That means pit pressure is not just wear-driven: some rows are forced by remaining legal compound options or by having delayed the mandatory second slick too long.
implementation_hint: Build stricter legality features than the current prior-slick-count heuristic: has mandatory second dry compound already been satisfied, number of distinct slick compounds already used this race, whether current compound repeats a previous slick, laps remaining versus feasible one-stop/two-stop legal paths, and dry-versus-wet regime overrides. Keep all path inference row-causal within each driver-race sequence.
expected_effect: Improves late-race and first-stop decisions where legality pressure dominates pure degradation, especially in dry races with limited prior slick diversity.
risk: Without actual weekend set-allocation data, some path features are approximate. Wet/intermediate races can break dry-rule assumptions unless explicitly gated.
